response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and real low-latency systems background (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic), both relevant to routing tile pipelines. However, the core requirement of modern C++ or Rust is weakly met: C++ exposure is brief and dated (a 7-month stint in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project), recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, and there is no navigation or routing systems experience. Worth a screen only if the team is open to a strong systems generalist ramping up on the primary language.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map) over many years
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work with async I/O and caching at Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++/Rust requirement not met; C++ experience is brief and roughly a decade old
No experience with navigation guidance, routing engines, or map/traffic data pipelines specifically
Recent roles are full-stack TypeScript product work, a shift away from systems-level engineering
